RE: Sale of Mortgage Loan(s) by Opes Advisors, A Division Of Flagstar Bank, Fsb 
Dear Sir/Madam:
Enclosed please find the original promissory note (The Note) and related collateral documents for the mortgage loan 
described in Schedule 1 below (the "Mortgage Loan"). Opes Advisors, A Division Of Flagstar Bank, Fsb has granted a 
security interest in the Mortgage Loan and the Note to Flagstar Bank, FSB ("Flagstar").
AmeriHome c/o Deutsche Bank * 
1761 East St. Andrew Place
Schedule 1

Michelle Ansaldi-Dunnell 
405 Shaw Avenue

The Note, and all other related documents, whether now or hereafter delivered to you, are to be held by you in trust (as 
bailee) for the benefit of Flagstar subject only to Flagstar's direction and control. Unless you return the enclosed Note and 
related documents immediately upon your receipt of same, you shall be deemed to have accepted the described bailment, and 
this bailee letter shall be binding upon you and your successors assigns.If you accept the Note for purchase, the applicable 
purchase funds shall be wire transferred with immediately available funds to Flagstar within twenty-one (21) business days 
as follows:
TO: Flagstar Bank FSB, Troy, MI 
ABA #: 27-247-1852 
Account #: 1558 
Credit: Warehouse Department 
Reference #: 82182-16848
For: Opes Advisors, A Division Of Flagstar Bank, Fsb Michelle Ansaldi-Dunnell 
ATTN: Rick Luckadoo, Warehousing (Telephone 517 817-1063)
* The above instructions must be included or the wire will be RETURNED!
Any Note which is not accepted by you should be returned to Flagstar, along with all other related documents, no later than 
twenty-one (21) business days from the date first written above, to the attention of Rick Luckadoo and/or Holly Stetz,
Flagstar Bank, FSB, Warehouse Lending, Mail Stop W-800, 5151 Corporate Drive, Troy, MI 48098.
The Note and related documents have not been assigned or transferred by Flagstar to any other party. Flagstar has no 
obligation to release its security interest in the Note and related documents unless Flagstar receives the purchase proceeds for 
the pledged Mortgage Loan. [Flagstar acknowledges that the purchase proceeds for the Mortgage Loan may be less than the 
Note amount set forth in this letter.] Flagstar will only release its security interest in the Mortgage Loan if the purchase 
proceeds are not reduced by adjustments or offsets unrelated to the Mortgage Loan. Subject to the foregoing, upon Flagstar’s 
receipt of the purchase proceeds, its security interest, claim, lien, encumbrance and any other interest that Flagstar has in such 
Mortgage Loan, including the Note and other documents evidencing or securing such Mortgage Loan, shall terminate 
without further action from any party, and Flagstar shall have no further claim with respect to such Mortgage Loan.
In no event shall the Note or any related documents be delivered to any third party, pledged, or otherwise dealt with by you 
until Flagstar has received payment as described above. Until such time or until the Note and related documents are safely 
returned to Flagstar’s possession, you are responsible for the care, custody, protection and safeguarding of the Note and 
related collateral documents and will be held accountable therefore.
You shall not honor any request or instruction from any third party regarding the Note and other collateral documents, unless 
you have received written consent of Flagstar to such new or variant instructions, or until Flagstar has received the applicable

purchase proceeds for the Mortgage Loan, as set forth above. This Agreement shall be governed by, and shall be interpreted 
and enforced in accordance with, the laws of the State of Michigan (without regard to choice of law principles).
Notwithstanding any other provision of this letter or any other document or agreements, Flagstar Bank reserves the right, 
exercisable at any time before you have paid Flagstar for the Mortgage Loan, to require, by written notice delivered to you in 
any legally effective manner, that you return the Note and other documents to Flagstar.
Please acknowledge receipt of this bailee letter and the Note by signing and returning a copy of this letter to Rick Luckadoo 
and/or Holly Stetz, Warehouse Lending, Flagstar Bank, FSB, 301 W. Michigan Ave., Jackson, MI 49201. Alternatively, you 
may email a PDF of the countersigned letter to rick.luckadoo@flagstar.com and/or holly.r.stetz@flagstar.com. Inquiries 
regarding this Bailee Letter may be directed to Rick Luckadoo at (517) 817-1063 or Holly Stetz at (517) 817-1098.
Very truly yours,
o
Flagstar Bank, FSB
By:___RickLuckadoo____
Its: First Vice-President
Any action involving this bailee letter or the Note shall be brought solely in a stat or federal court sitting in the State of 
Michigan. Your signature below indicates your submission to the jurisdiction of any such court for the purpose of 
adjudicating any such action and your waiver of any objection to the venue of any such action commenced in any such court.
Received and accepted this____________day of____________________ , 2019
AmeriHome c/o Deutsche Bank *

Dear Consumer:  
 
Please find enclosed a copy of the Automated Valuation Model(s) your lender may have used during your recent loan
application.  You are receiving copies of these documents because of a change to the Equal Credit Opportunity Act 
(ECOA).  
 
What is an Automated Valuation Model (AVM)?  An AVM is a computer generated estimate of a value for a 
residential property as of a specific date.  
 
How does an AVM differ from an appraisal?  The most significant difference is that an appraisal report is complet
by a licensed appraiser who has inspected the property and comparable sales.  
 
Why do lenders use AVMs?  AVMs are used to assist lenders with reducing valuation risk on their real estate 
loans.  Specifically, if the variance between the AVM and the appraisal is outside a lender’s threshold, the lender may
require a senior underwriter or in-house appraiser to conduct an additional review of the appraisal.  
 
Typical Components of An AVM:  There are many different AVMs in the marketplace and some may or may not 
contain all of the following components.  
l Estimated Market Value: A value estimate based on the computer analysis of the subject property and nearby 
sales  
l Valuation Date: The date the estimated market value was determined  
l Confidence Score: A predictive measurement of the accuracy of the estimated market value; higher confidenc
scores generally correlate to higher accuracy levels  
l Value Ranges (High & Low): The estimated market value range for a property based on the computer analysis 
available market data and confidence score  
l Subject Property Information: Data obtained from the tax assessor  
l Sales History (Subject & Nearby Sales): Data obtained from the Recorder’s Office  
l Market Sales: Sales of properties near the subject property that are similar in land use  
The End User of the AVM is the lender and not you, the consumer.  As a consumer, you should not rely solely on
the Valuation Service to make a financial decision, such as the purchase of a home.  If you have any question
regarding the AVM(s) used in connection with your loan, please contact your lender directly.
